actionLibraryList.title: The World's Greatest Stretch​
actionLibraryList.context: Keep your core engaged and pelvis in a neutral position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749634409876.jpg","context":"1.Place both hands on the floor shoulder-width apart and bend one elbow.
2.Keep the back leg straight.
3.Keep the front thigh parallel to the floor."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749634414396.jpg","context":"1.Keep the knee behind the toes.
2.Rotate the upper body toward the front leg and extend the same-side arm upward."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Inhale to lengthen the spine, exhale to deepen the stretch.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: You’ll feel a stretch in the hips, thoracic spine, and shoulders.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid compensatory rotation in the lumbar spine or inward collapse of the knee.

actionLibraryList.title: In-place Inchworm
actionLibraryList.context: Ensure the entire palm remains in contact with the floor.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749700102676.jpg","context":"1.Always maintain the feeling of pushing your heels back into the ground.\n2.Keep your knees fully extended at all times.\n3.Ensure the entire palm remains in contact with the floor."}]
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Focus on the stretch in your legs

actionLibraryList.title: Barbell Squat
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with your feet hip-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
2.Hold the barbell and place it across your upper traps, just below the neck.
3.Keeping your chest up, drive your hips back.
4.Allow your knees to move forward in the direction your toes are pointing.
5.Aim to achieve 90 degrees at both knees, with your eyes forward.
6.Driving through your heels, lift your body back to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Lock the pulleys to the footboard end with the barbell attachment.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Squat until your thighs are parallel to or slightly below the floor.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep your chin up, chest out, back straight, core tight, and your toes in line with your knees.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Keep your breath steady. Inhale as you descend, and exhale as you ascend.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Initiate the ascending movement by engaging your glutes.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Load the barbell onto your traps and shoulders.
2.Keep your back straight and your glutes engaged.
3.Slightly bend your knees.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750230418406.jpg"},{"context":"1.Lower into a squat with an upright back until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
2.Align your knees and toes, and avoid letting your knees cave in.
3.Slightly rotate your feet outward.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750230423096.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you rise, inhale as you lower.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your chin up, chest open, back straight, core tight, and your toes in line with your knees.

actionLibraryList.title: Handle Lateral Split Squat
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Hold the handle, keeping your torso upright.
2.Stand with feet about 1.5 times shoulder-width apart.
3.Bend one leg into a lunge, keeping the knee aligned with your toes.
4.Drive through the heel of the lunging leg to return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Set the handle to the floor position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Engage your core and keep your back straight. Slowly hinge at the hips as you lower yourself down.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Step out, bend your knee, and push your hips back, lowering into a side lunge. Press through the heel of your lunging leg to return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Make sure the movement is controlled and avoid snapping your knee straight. Control the movement throughout.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale as you exert force, inhale as you return.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Targets the quadriceps, hamstrings, glutes, and inner thighs
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750235586664.jpg","context":"1.Stand with feet about 1.5 times shoulder-width apart.
2.Keep your back straight and look forward."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750235590794.jpg","context":"1.Keep your knees aligned with your toes.
2.Slowly hinge at the hips as you squat.
3.Maintain a straight back."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Inhale as you squat, exhale as you rise.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: The exercise targets the quadriceps, hamstrings, glutes, and inner thigh muscles.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Maintain control over the movement at all times.

actionLibraryList.title: Standing Dual-Handle Reverse-Grip Lateral Raise
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, core engaged.
2.Grip the handles with a reverse grip, arms relaxed at your sides.
3.Keep elbows slightly bent and raise both arms laterally to shoulder height or slightly above.
4.Control the movement, keeping both upward and downward phases smooth and stable.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748143386444.jpg","context":"1.Grip the handles with a reverse grip (palms facing forward).\n2.Shoulders down, arms relaxed at your sides."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748143391141.jpg","context":"1.Raise both arms laterally to shoulder height or slightly above."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you lift, inhale as you lower.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Focus on activating the middle delts.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid using the traps to compensate.

actionLibraryList.title: V-ups
actionLibraryList.context: Lie on your back on a mat with your legs extended and your hands placed behind your head or by your sides. Engage your core as you lift both your upper body and legs, bringing them toward the center of your body, fully contracting your abs at the top. Pause at the top, then lower back down with control. Exhale as you lift, inhale as you lower. Avoid using momentum and keep the movement smooth and steady throughout.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750056642227.jpg","context":"1.Lie on your back on a yoga mat or flat surface with your legs straight and together."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750056645354.jpg","context":"1.Extend both arms straight forward, reaching toward your toes."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you lift, inhale as you lower.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: You should feel a strong contraction in your abs.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid using momentum throughout, keeping the movement smooth and controlled.

actionLibraryList.title: Bent-Over Dual-Handle Reverse Fly
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Bent forward with a neutral spine and slightly bent knees.
2.Hold a handle in each hand, arms hanging naturally, palms facing each other.
3.Raise the arms in an outward arc until they are parallel to the floor.
4.Pause briefly at the top, feeling the shoulder muscle contraction
5.Slowly lower the handles back to the starting position under control.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Lock the pulley block to the floor with two handles attached
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Hinge at your hips while keeping your back straight.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Raise the handles outward and upward from your chest until they reach shoulder height.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Don't hold your breath. Exhale as you pull the cable up, inhale, and gently lower it back to the starting position
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Fully stretch and engage your rear deltoids and briefly hold the contraction at the top.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Relax your shoulders.
2.Bend your knees slightly.
3.Hinge at your hips while keeping your back straight.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748145517622.jpg"},{"context":"1.Avoid excessive scapular squeezing.
2.Keep the arms at roughly a 90° angle to the torso.
3.Maintain a straight back.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748145521391.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale while lifting, inhale while lowering.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage the posterior deltoids, abduct the arms horizontally until full contraction.

actionLibraryList.title: Barbell Single-Leg Hip Thrust
actionLibraryList.context: Drive through the heel of your grounded foot to lift your hips. Raise your hips until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Set the cable to bottom position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Position your upper back against the bench, with your shoulder blades resting on the edge. Place one foot flat on the ground, with the knee bent at about a 90-degree angle.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Drive through the heel of your grounded foot, pushing your hips upward. Lift your hips until your body forms a straight line from your shoulders to your knee.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Ensure that your back stays neutral. Focus on squeezing your glutes throughout the exercise, especially at the top of the thrust.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale while exerting force, inhale while returning.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Targets the glutes, hamstrings, and core.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749091493231.jpg","context":"1.Position your upper back against the bench, with your shoulder blades resting on the edge.\n2.Place one foot flat on the ground, with your knee bent at a 90-degree angle."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749091497388.jpg","context":"1.Push through the heel of your grounded foot to lift your hips.\n2.Raise your hips until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale while exerting force, inhale while returning.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Targets the glute muscles, hamstrings, and core muscles.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your back straight. Focus on squeezing your glutes throughout the exercise, especially at the top of the movement.
